title: Postmortem Mouse Tissue Processing (Necropsy)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5juok","text":"Paper abstract: CRISPR-Cas9 gene-editing technology has revolutionised the creation of precise and permanent modifications to DNA, enabling the generation of diverse animal models for investigating potential treatments. Here, we provide a protocol for the use of CRISPR-Cas9 to create murine models of Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy (DMD) along with a step-by-step guide for their phenotypic and molecular characterisation. The experimental procedures include CRISPR microinjection of embryos, molecular testing at the DNA, RNA, and protein levels, forelimb grip strength testing, immunostaining and serum creatine kinase (CK) testing. We further provide suggestions for analysis and interpretation of the generated data, as well as the limitations of our approach. These protocols are designed for researchers who intend on generating and using mouse models to study DMD as well as those seeking a detailed framework of phenotyping to contribute to the broader landscape of genetic disorder investigations.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"it1n","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2il7p","text":"Protocol summary: A template for the steps preceding downstream molecular analyses of heart and skeletal muscle tissues. All procedures should be conducted in accordance with the ethical guidelines outlined by your animal ethics committee in your local institution.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2tlot","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"652de","text":"Wear proper PPE (gloves, safety goggles, enclosed shoes and lab coat) and prepare solvents in a chemical fume hood. Dispose used solvents or waste material in an appropriate biohazard waste containers.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"3ufei","text":" CO2 chamber.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":3,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"audoa","text":" Liquid nitrogen (N2).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":19,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cbb4k","text":" Isopentane, isopentane container.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"26bai","text":" 70% ethanol.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"43ulh","text":" Dissection tools (i.e., scalpels, scissors, tweezers, large forceps).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4ovhl","text":" Cork disc for cryostat use (20 mm x 3 mm), cork holder.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9mpt2","text":" Gum tragacanth (Sigma-Aldrich","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Euthanise the mouse using an AEC-approved method. Sacrifice mouse by CO2 exposure followed by cervical dislocation

For downstream molecular analysis (DNA, RNA and Protein), cut a small portion of each isolated tissue while the remaining tissues will be subsequently frozen in liquid N2-cooled isopentane for cryo-sectioning. Ensure the muscle is in its normal physiological orientation without stretching.
Prepare the gum tragacanth mixture by mixing 10% (w/v) into water in a small beaker. Continue stirring and leave to set until a slurry forms.

Place a conservative amount of slurry on the cork pads suitable for tissue mounting. Mount the tissue sections on to the cork to ensure at least 2/3rds of the section remain upright and oriented for a transverse section.
Using pre-chilled forceps, lower the specimen and cork into the isopentane and suspend for the appropriate time (20s for smaller tissues, 30s for heart and quad), depending on muscle size.
Transfer specimen immediately to dry ice and label to store in a -80°C freezer until sectioning.
Transport tissues on dry ice to the cryostat to prevent thawing.
Set the cryostat chamber to -20°C to -24°C and let frozen samples equilibrate for 30 min.
Apply a uniform thin layer of OCT to the specimen disc. With the tissue freeze mounted on the cork, place the sample at the correct orientation.
Mount the specimen onto the specimen head in the cryostat, ensuring good contact.
Set the chamber temperature to -21°C to -24°C and the chamber head temperature to -15°C.
Adjust thickness settings (7-10 µm) and trim the block as needed.
Collect sections on pre-warmed positively charged microscope slides by pressing them onto the sections.
Inventory and archive slides at -20°C for long-term storage or proceed to staining.
Cut sections perpendicular to the direction of myofiber orientation.

Pour isopentane in a metal container and place within a larger container containing liquid nitrogen. Chill isopentane in liquid nitrogen until white solid precipitate is visible at the bottom of the container. The optimal temperature range for isopentane is around -150°C. Once within this temperature range, solid white pebbles of frozen isopentane will form at the bottom. Avoid freezing artifacts by ensuring freezing occurs after this stage. In the event that the isopentane freezes solid, allow it to thaw and then re-chill to freezing temperatures before using it again.
From each mouse, we usually isolate the quadriceps, triceps, heart and tibialis anterior.
creator: Jayshen Arudkumar
